### 2) The price is **implausibly low**
This listing is **US$1,900 / about AU$2,648** for an **18-inch Ultra 9 / RTX 5090 / 2TB** Legion 9i.

By comparison, Australian retail references for similar Legion 9 Gen 10 configs are far higher:
- Harvey Norman lists an **RTX 5080 / 64GB / 2TB** Legion at **AU$7,698**.

citeturn769408search8
- Kogan lists a **RTX 5090** Legion at **AU$7,867.66**.

This is the **HP Spectre x360 16 (2024)** listing at about **AU$2,299**, advertised as **brand new**, with **Intel Core Ultra 7 155H, 32GB LPDDR5x RAM, 1TB SSD, 16-inch 2.8K OLED touch display, Windows 11 Pro, and RTX 4050 graphics**.
The listing is for a **refurbished Scar 18** with **i9-14900HX, RTX 4080, 32GB RAM, 2TB SSD** at **AU$3,989.05**, sold by the same established AU refurb seller.

The listing shows **Ryzen AI 9 HX 370, RTX 4070, 32GB, 2TB, 16-inch 4K OLED touch** at **AU$3,514.05**, again from **treasure_pc_online**, with certified-refurb status and a 12-month Asus Australia pickup/return warranty.

Meanwhile Dell Australia’s own Area-51 page shows the **RTX 5090 configuration at AU$7,198.40** and the platform is a very large machine at up to **4.34 kg**, with upgradeable memory/storage documented by Dell support.
The HP Official Store listing on eBay AU shows the Spectre x360 16 **32GB/1TB/RTX 4050** at about **AU$4,124**, which is premium pricing for hardware that is not premium-workstation class anymore.
On the **new local** side, most realistic under-A$4.5k options are either **RTX 4080 / 12GB** machines discounted into range, or brand-new **RTX 5070 Ti / 12GB** systems.

The best value clusters are:
- **Local new 18-inch RTX 4080** around **A$3,998–4,499**,
- **Local custom-barebones 17.3-inch RTX 5070 Ti** around **A$3,499**,
- **Imported/refurb 17–18 inch RTX 4090** around **A$3.6k–4.3k raw landed before any uncertain extra charges**.

In this segment, refurb/high-confidence used can be the difference between:
- **RTX 4080 / 12GB new**, and
- **RTX 4090 / 16GB used/refurb**  
while staying inside the same A$3.8k–4.3k budget zone.

Within PB Tech’s MSI 18-inch range, the jump to RTX 5080-class 18-inch models is very large, landing around **A$5.7k–A$6.1k**, which makes this Crosshair look like the more pragmatic value tier rather than a compromised near-flagship.

**Better than the old ZBook on modern speed; weaker than it on capacity; and probably worse value than the Crosshair.** The PB Tech Katana 17 HX you linked is **A$2,508.56**, with an **i7-14650HX**, **RTX 5060 Laptop GPU 8GB**, **16GB DDR5-5600 (2x8GB)**, **1TB SSD**, and a **17.3-inch QHD 240Hz** panel.

The Crosshair 18 HX AI model you surfaced is about **A$3,446.74**, but for that extra money you move to a much stronger package: **Core Ultra 9 275HX**, **32GB RAM**, **18-inch QHD+ 240Hz**, **RTX 5070 8GB**, **798 AI TOPS**, **100% DCI-P3** on the display, and a **2-year warranty**.
Same broad class of “new gaming laptop,” but this config is only **16GB / RTX 5060 8GB** at about **A$2.5k**, which is a risky amount to spend if the goal is longevity for dev + AI-adjacent work.

**Yes — as a cheap, brute-force desktop-replacement refurb, this is actually quite compelling.**  
At **A$814**, it gives you a **17.3-inch chassis**, **64GB RAM**, a **Quadro/RTX 5000 GPU**, and a **12-month return-to-base warranty** with a **30-day change-of-mind period**.
Dell Australia shows an **Alienware 18 Area-51 with RTX 5090** at **AU$7,198.40** for one official config, and Amazon shows a same-spec **64GB / 2TB / RTX 5090** model at **US$4,020**.

At **A$3,599**, the Mike PC listing gives you an i9-14900HX, RTX 4090 16GB, 32GB DDR5, 2TB SSD, and a 16-inch 2560×1600 240Hz display, and it is explicitly described as a **demo unit** with **1-year Lenovo Australia manufacturer warranty**.
Lenovo’s own AU search snippets show a **Legion Pro 7i Gen 10 with RTX 5070 Ti at A$4,499**, while Centre Com snippets show the **Legion Pro 7i Gen 10 RTX 5080 at A$5,999**.

**Bottom line:**  
At **A$3,599**, this is a **strong price for a premium RTX 4090 Legion**, especially with 32GB/2TB and upgradeable RAM/storage.
